storyTopic: As the exhibition opens, Amina has to introduce her grandmother publicly and chooses to speak in a way that resists simplification, using emphasis to reclaim what the official labels leave out.
setting: Opening night at the museum, moving between the gallery floor, Amina's short speech, and a quiet final exchange after the guests leave

mainCharacters.name: Amina
mainCharacters.description: 17-year-old student, more self-possessed now, determined to speak honestly without turning her family into a performance

mainCharacters.name: Nadia Karim
mainCharacters.description: Amina's grandmother, composed and exacting, moved by truthfulness more than praise

mainCharacters.name: Ms Hargreaves
mainCharacters.description: museum curator who listens carefully when she realises the official framing is incomplete

toneAndStyle: Poised, expressive B2 prose with rhetorical control and emotional precision. The emphasis structures should appear in speech, inner reflection, and narration with a real dramatic function, not as isolated grammar examples.
emotionalArc: tension -> self-command -> public truth -> quiet release
notes: This chapter should use emphasis structures to show Amina gaining rhetorical control. Include at least one meaningful example each of an it-cleft, a wh-cleft, and inversion after a negative adverbial. The focus is not performance for its own sake, but reclaiming narrative accuracy. The ending should be emotionally understated: what matters is that the grandmother feels seen, not celebrated in a simplistic way.
